The question is asking to calculate the duration in years from the first race to the last race. To find this, we need to identify the earliest and latest years in the data. To determine the order of years, we can use the f_sort_column(year) operation with the order of "small to large" to arrange the years from the earliest to the latest. Once sorted, we can subtract the earliest year (first row) from the latest year (last row) to find the duration in years.

The question is asking to calculate the duration in years between the first race year and the last race year. Since each row represents a different year, we need to find the minimum (first) and maximum (last) values in the "year" column to determine the time span. We can use the f_min(year) and f_max(year) operations to find the first and last years, then subtract them to calculate the duration.

The first race was in 1926 and the last race was in 1939. To calculate the number of years between these two races, subtract the first year from the last year (1939 - 1926). Therefore, the duration from the first race until the last race was 13 years.

judge_thought: Explanation: The question asks for the duration in years from the first race until the last race. Looking at the original table, the first race occurred in 1926 (row 1) and the last race occurred in 1939 (row 13). To calculate the duration, subtract the year of the first race from the year of the last race: 1939 - 1926 = 13 years. Therefore, the Prediction Answer of 13 is correct.
